A threc-halled house, where the northern hall is absent, is
named Hiranyandbha and fortunate. Similarly, Sukfelra is one where
the eastern hall is absent and leads to prosperity. One without the
southern hall is designated as Culft, which leads to loss of wealth.
Lastly a house devoid of the western hall is named Pak^aghna and is
destructive to children, and the cause of hatred.

By suitable adjustment of the lines in the first four types of
houses we can get these three-hailed houses.]
In the following verses the author described six types of two-
halled houses.

is known as SiddhSrtha; one with the western and northern halls
